title: C# Basic — Paper 10
description: C# concepts: arrays, variables, strings, nullable types, type casting, inheritance basics, enums, structs, methods, and ref/out parameters.

questions.text: What is the difference between `Array.Sort()` and LINQ `OrderBy()` in C#?

options.text: Array.Sort sorts in place; OrderBy returns a new sorted IEnumerable
OrderBy modifies the original array; Array.Sort creates a copy
Array.Sort is only for strings; OrderBy is for integers

questions.text: How do you initialize a 2D array with values in C#?
What does `Array.Clear(arr, 0, arr.Length)` do in C#?

options.text: Removes all elements, making the array empty
Sets all elements in the range to their type's default value (0, false, null)
Sorts the array in ascending order

questions.text: What does `char` represent in C# and what is its size?
What is the difference between `short` and `int` in C# in terms of storage?
What does the compiler infer the type to be for `var list = new List<int>()`?
What does `string.Concat("Hello", " ", "World")` return in C#?
What is the result of `"abcdef".Substring(2, 3)` in C#?
What does `string.Join(", ", new[] {"a", "b", "c"})` return in C#?
What is the `??=` operator and give an example of its use in C#?

options.text: It is a null check only; does not assign
It assigns the right value only if the left variable is null: `name ??= "Unknown";`
It compares two nullable values and returns the larger
It converts a null value to false

questions.text: What is the behavior of accessing `.Value` on a null `int?` in C#?

options.text: Compiles but always returns a default

questions.text: How do you safely check if a nullable has a value before using it in C#?
What is the difference between `Convert.ToInt32(null)` and `int.Parse(null)` in C#?
What is the purpose of `checked` keyword in arithmetic in C#?

options.text: Enables overflow checking so OverflowException is thrown on integer overflow

questions.text: What happens when you cast a `long` with a value greater than `int.MaxValue` to `int` in unchecked context?

options.text: The value wraps around (overflow), giving an unexpected result
The value is clamped to int.MaxValue

questions.text: What is `virtual` dispatch in C# and how does the CLR resolve it at runtime?

options.text: The CLR calls the method based on the declared (compile-time) type of the variable
The CLR resolves the call based on the actual (runtime) type of the object, calling the most-derived override
The CLR randomly selects which override to call for performance
The CLR always calls the base class version first

questions.text: What does `sealed` do when applied to an overriding method in a derived class in C#?

options.text: Prevents the method from being called outside the class
Prevents any further subclass from overriding this method

questions.text: Which statement about interfaces in C# is correct?

options.text: A class can inherit from multiple interfaces and one class simultaneously
A class can only implement one interface
Interfaces can have private instance constructors

questions.text: What is the difference between `is` and `as` for type checking in C#?

options.text: `is` returns bool; `as` attempts a reference cast and returns null on failure

questions.text: What is the result of `(Season)(int)Season.Winter + 1` if Winter=3 and the next value doesn't exist in the enum?

options.text: Returns the integer value 4 cast as Season, even if no member has that value

questions.text: How do you specify the starting value of an enum member in C#?
What does `Enum.TryParse("North", out Direction d)` do in C#?

options.text: Throws an exception if the string is not a valid enum name
Returns true and sets d if "North" is a valid Direction; returns false otherwise
Converts the string to integer first

questions.text: What is a `record struct` introduced in C# 10?

options.text: A record type that is a reference type with value semantics
A value-type record with auto-generated equality, Deconstruct, and ToString based on its properties
A struct that can inherit from other structs
A struct with only static members

questions.text: What is the main advantage of using a struct over a class for a `Point` type (x, y coordinates) in C#?

options.text: Structs avoid heap allocation for small, short-lived data, reducing GC pressure
Structs can have more methods than classes
Structs support interface implementation unlike classes

questions.text: What does `void Swap(ref int a, ref int b)` allow when called?

options.text: Returns two values via out parameters
Swaps the values of the two caller variables directly, since both are passed by reference
Swaps only if both values are positive
Creates copies and swaps the copies

questions.text: Can a C# method have both `ref` and `out` parameters simultaneously?

options.text: Yes, a method can have different parameters marked as ref or out independently
Only if all parameters are ref or all are out

questions.text: What distinguishes method overloading resolution when both an `int` and `double` overload exist and a `float` argument is passed?

options.text: The `double` overload is chosen because float is implicitly widened to double
The compiler picks the overload alphabetically

questions.text: Which of the following correctly declares a method that returns multiple values using tuples in C#?
What is the `in` parameter modifier useful for in performance-sensitive code?

options.text: Passing large structs by reference without copying while guaranteeing they won't be modified
Allowing null values in value types

questions.text: What happens when you try to use `ref` with a property (not a field or variable) as the argument in C#?

options.text: It works fine; properties are treated like variables
A compile-time error occurs because properties are not lvalues and cannot be passed as ref
The compiler automatically uses the backing field
